release_review.summary: Redeven v0.8.2 restores the Code App controller capability nonce and signed proxy.runtime@1 WebSocket frame limit across browser navigation, includes the Flower subagent task panel refinement, and makes Flower terminal process completion consistent with the canonical Floret activity timeline. Terminal process read, write, and terminate now acknowledge completion only after the idempotent Floret settlement, tool-result persistence, settlement projection, and canonical timeline replacement succeed. These changes preserve Runtime Service protocol redeven-runtime-v1, compatibility epoch 6, and the matched v0.8.1 Desktop and Runtime minimum pair because they do not change Runtime Service RPC type IDs or payloads, terminal attach/history wire contracts, startup reports, or Desktop lifecycle control.
release_review.same_window_rationale: Redeven v0.8.2 keeps the v0.8.1 compatibility window because its browser bridge handoff and Flower settlement correction do not add a Runtime Service capability or wire field. The terminal process read, write, and terminate endpoints remain the existing authenticated Local UI and Code App HTTP routes with unchanged paths, request fields, terminal snapshot schema, and error envelope; v0.8.2 only waits for an in-flight settlement and returns an existing error response instead of exposing a terminal snapshot before canonical publication succeeds. Desktop v0.8.1 can consume the delayed but unchanged v0.8.2 terminal response, while Desktop v0.8.2 continues to consume the terminal snapshot and canonical Flower live-event shapes already emitted by Runtime v0.8.1. The epoch-6 terminal attach/history contract, sys.ping, Local UI health, startup reports, runtime attach/start, and SSH bootstrap remain unchanged.
checked_surfaces: Remote Flowersec Direct and Tunnel connections explicitly require TLS, Local UI Direct connections explicitly allow plaintext only for canonical loopback authorities, and the Docker integration client uses the same loopback-only exception

Flowersec Go and TypeScript v0.21.1 and Floe Webapp v0.37.2 are consumed only from published artifacts with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, package-manager link, workspace alias, or local sibling path wiring

Acknowledged Yamux liveness, 64 KiB encrypted-record chunks, WebSocket watermarks, Yamux stream and receive-memory limits, bounded RPC workers and queues, and the Flowersec write-queue budget change transport resource behavior without changing Redeven RPC type IDs or payload schemas

Flowersec v0.21.1 consumes authenticated handshake state once, rejects proxy redirects for caller handling, binds browser proxy origin to validated source context, applies one-chunk Service Worker credit backpressure with legacy compatibility, bounds outbound and proxy queues, propagates cancellation and session termination, and retains ConnectArtifact v1, E2EE record framing, Yamux wire behavior, RPC type IDs, and proxy.runtime@1

The injected Code App runtime bridge restores a controller-issued capability nonce and signed proxy.runtime@1 WebSocket frame limit from the isolated app origin after location replacement and refresh, rejects malformed or oversized limits in favor of the existing 32 MiB product cap, and preserves the no-nonce legacy path when those values are absent for compatibility with previous control-plane releases

Redeven blocks only Content-Security-Policy, Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only, and X-Frame-Options on proxied product surfaces; X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, COOP, COEP, and CORP continue through Flowersec's default response policy

Env App reconnects use refreshable ArtifactSource acquire functions and stable reconnect config objects so credentials remain one-use, an in-flight retry promise is reused across unexpected disconnects, and trace context survives hard reconnects without changing session metadata or Runtime Service envelopes

Flower preserves the flower-model-directed-tool-concurrency boundary from published Floret v0.4.0: model-selected tools may execute concurrently, user-facing actions remain in a serialized approval queue, and Permission snapshots, transcript ordering, cancellation, audit, control-signal, projection recovery, token-budget, deletion-fencing, and activity-summary boundaries remain internal to Flower and Floret

Code App terminal process read, write, and terminate keep their authenticated HTTP paths, query and body fields, terminal snapshot response shape, and existing API error envelope; v0.8.2 serializes one idempotent Floret settlement, withholds terminal snapshots until tool-result persistence and canonical timeline replacement succeed, and reports settlement failures through the existing HTTP error path

Flower terminal process polling remains an output, sequence, and process-control channel: Flower and Env App row status continues to come from the existing canonical activity timeline, so process snapshot status does not become a new Runtime Service compatibility field or an alternate lifecycle source

Desktop v0.8.1 with Runtime v0.8.2 receives the same terminal process payload after stronger acknowledgement ordering, and Desktop v0.8.2 with Runtime v0.8.1 uses the existing terminal snapshot and Flower live-event shapes without requiring new fields, event kinds, or RPC type IDs

Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1 and compatibility_epoch remains 6 because terminal.attach still requires an ordered request generation plus an explicit atomic history boundary and terminal.history still requires retained-floor, committed-coverage, snapshot-end, history-generation, reset, and truncation metadata
The Runtime consumes only the published github.com/floegence/floeterm/terminal-go v0.4.26 module with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, or local sibling path wiring

Env App consumes only the published @floegence/floeterm-terminal-web 0.5.19 package and maps the Redeven attach/history responses into the released two-phase coordinator contract without a product-local recovery state machine
Runtime registers each terminal sink and captures its committed history boundary under one manager/session lock order, returns zero explicitly, and routes only later source sequences to that sink

Env App assigns each attach RPC a transport-scoped monotonic generation; Runtime rejects stale generations before boundary capture, lets duplicate requests for one generation share one session-owned cancellable activation result, completes and cancels pending activation when the session starts closing or its sink, session, or Manager closes, rejects closed sinks, and conditionally rolls activation failure back only when that attachment still owns the mapping

Env App begins coordinator attachment before subscribing to live output, completes it with the Runtime boundary, and retains output produced during the attach round trip; missing or malformed boundaries fail closed as update-required contract errors
Initial terminal input becomes available only after the history baseline has crossed the terminal parser completion fence; retry and degraded recovery after that baseline remain non-blocking

Missing, malformed, stale-generation, reset, and retention-truncated history contracts fail or rebase through structured coordinator states instead of inferring coverage from retained chunks or rendering raw internal errors

Desktop and Runtime minimum versions remain matched at v0.8.1 so a new Env App cannot silently attach to an older Runtime that omits required coverage metadata; the published v0.8.0 epoch 5 window remains outside this contract
Local transport and credential boundaries, execute permission enforcement, managed Runtime process reconciliation, Activity surface lifecycle, and the Floret review boundary remain intact

Desktop attach/start, structured startup reports, sys.ping, Local UI health, Env App maintenance routes, gateway lifecycle, SSH Runtime bootstrap, installer discovery, and provider request shaping remain otherwise unchanged
